channel.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: politics
channel.description: Get the latest news online at Major Digest, the top online news resource for the most reliable news sources. Stay informed about the world around you!

image.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: politics

item.title: Trump fires the director of the Consumer Financial Protection Bureau
item.description: PALM BEACH, Fla. (AP) — President Donald Trump has fired the director of the Consumer Financial Protection Bureau, Rohit Chopra, in the latest purge of a Biden administration holdover.
item.dc:creator: JOSH BOAK, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: California city's massive $130M deficit threatens dangerous cuts to its firefighting capacity
item.description: The city of Oakland will lose 30% of its firefighting capacity in the next two weeks due to the city’s $129.8 million budget deficit.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Democrats are electing a new leader as their party struggles to repair its brand
item.description: Democrats are poised to elect a new leader in a low-profile Democratic National Committee election that could have big implications for the party’s future.
item.dc:creator: Steve Peoples, Associated Press, Local 4

item.title: Things to know about how Trump's policies target transgender people
item.description: President Donald Trump has targeted the rights of transgender people in executive orders in his first two weeks back in office.
item.dc:creator: Geoff Mulvihill, Associated Press, KPRC 2

item.title: Second federal judge orders temporary pause to Trump administration efforts to freeze funding
item.description: BOSTON (AP) — A second federal judge on Friday ordered a temporary pause in Trump administration efforts to freeze federal funding in the latest twist over the spending of trillions of dollars in grants and loans.
item.dc:creator: MICHAEL CASEY, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Political parties explain Hillsborough County's conservative flip
item.description: As of Thursday, Hillsborough County records showed the number of active registered Republicans was bigger than the number of registered Democrats. The difference as of Friday was 307 voters.
item.dc:creator: Ty Russell, WFLA, NBC

item.title: Woman who wanted to kill GOP leaders said she was inspired by CEO's slaying, prosecutors say
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — A Massachusetts woman who told police that she went to the U.S. Capitol to kill members of President Donald Trump's cabinet said she was influenced by Luigi Mangione, the man charged with fatally shooting the CEO of UnitedHealthcare, prosecutors said in a court filing.
item.dc:creator: MICHAEL KUNZELMAN, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Trump targets FAA diversity efforts in plane crash probe despite no evidence they played any role
item.description: NEW YORK (AP) — President Donald Trump wasted little time this week trying to assign blame for the nation’s deadliest air disaster in more than two decades. Among his chief targets: An FAA diversity hiring initiative he suggested had undermined the agency’s effectiveness.
item.dc:creator: MELISSA GOLDIN, ALI SWENSON and ALEXANDRA OLSON, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: The DNC's outgoing chair says Democrats should have stuck with Joe Biden in 2024
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — As the Democratic National Committee prepares to elect a new chair, its departing leader says Democrats should have stuck with Joe Biden in the 2024 race.
item.dc:creator: MEG KINNARD, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: What to know about the legal challenges to Trump’s executive actions
item.description: President Trump’s torrent of executive actions has spurred a flood of legal challenges seeking to thwart the president’s efforts to reshape U.S. policy in his first days back in the White House. From Seattle to Boston and Concord, N.H., to Washington, D.C.
item.dc:creator: Ella Lee, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: More migrants deported under Biden, Baja state official says
item.description: Tijuana Mayor Ismael Burgueño Ruiz says he will not lift an emergency declaration he made last month as a way to prepare for mass deportations from the United States.
item.dc:creator: Salvador Rivera, NewsNation

item.title: White House aide says spending freeze isn't being rescinded despite memo
item.description: The White House is claiming the Wednesday move by the Office of Management and Budget (OMB) rescinding a controversial order that froze a wide swath of federal financial assistance is not actually an end to curbing government spending. “This is NOT a rescission of the federal funding freeze.
item.dc:creator: Alex Gangitano, NewsNation

item.title: Senate confirms Doug Burgum as interior secretary after Trump tasked him to boost drilling
item.description: The Senate confirmed Doug Burgum as interior secretary late Thursday after President Donald Trump tapped the North Dakota billionaire to spearhead the Republican administration's ambitions to boost fossil fuel production. The vote was 79-18.
item.dc:creator: MATTHEW BROWN, Associated Press, NewsNation

item.title: Gabbard battles senators over Snowden, Syria in fiery hearing: 5 takeaways
item.description: Tulsi Gabbard, President Trump’s pick to be the next director of national intelligence, endured a difficult hearing before the Senate Intelligence Committee on Thursday as lawmakers pressed her on her views on Edward Snowden, spying, Syria and Russia.
item.dc:creator: Al Weaver, NewsNation

item.title: President Trump sounds the same. His White House
item.description: NEW YORK (AP) — The marathon Q&A sessions are back, along with the cream Oval Office rug and the Diet Coke button on the Resolute Desk. So, too, are the late-night social media posts that ricochet across the globe, the barrage of executive orders and press conferences filled with falsehoods.
item.dc:creator: JILL COLVIN, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Senate Democrats revolt against Trump in fallout from foreign aid freeze
item.description: Senate Democrats are starting to revolt against President Trump’s agenda on Capitol Hill amid fallout from an initial freeze on all foreign assistance, warning Republicans that failure to work together will incur consequences. Sen. Chris Coons (D-Del.) on Thursday reversed support for Rep.
item.dc:creator: Laura Kelly, Fox 5

item.title: Watch live: Gabbard makes case to lead national intelligence at confirmation hearing
item.description: Former Hawaii Rep. Tulsi Gabbard (D-Hawaii) is testifying Thursday morning before the Senate Select Committee on Intelligence to make her case as President Trump's pick for director of national intelligence.
item.dc:creator: The Hill Staff, NewsNation

item.title: Watch live: Kash Patel faces senators at confirmation hearing
item.description: Kash Patel, President Trump's controversial nominee to head the FBI, will face senators Thursday morning during a confirmation hearing.
item.dc:creator: The Hill Staff, NewsNation

item.title: Gabbard, RFK Jr., Patel hearings set to consume Capitol Hill
item.description: Hearing-mania is set to consume Capitol Hill on Thursday as a trio of President Trump's top allies appear for high-stakes confirmation showdowns with senators that could make or break their chances of getting across the finish line. Tulsi Gabbard, Robert F. Kennedy Jr.
item.dc:creator: Al Weaver, NewsNation

item.title: Democrats leave 'emergency' meeting vowing ‘street fight’ in response to Trump actions
item.description: House Democrats launched a multilateral effort Wednesday to combat the flood of executive actions from President Trump, vowing to punch back through legislation, lawsuits and a blitz of counter-messaging.
item.dc:creator: Mike Lillis, NewsNation

item.title: Trump's pulling a U-turn on EVs, but not much has changed
item.description: President Donald Trump has charted a new course for electric vehicle policy in the U.S. While the direction is clear, the details aren't.
item.dc:creator: Camila Domonoske, MPR News

item.title: Proposed trims to Minnesota’s disability waiver program concern people reliant on services
item.description: In his new budget, Gov. Tim Walz recommends restraining growth in the state’s disability waiver program — one of the best funded of its kind in the country. That has sparked worry despite Walz assurances that access and quality won’t suffer.
item.dc:creator: Peter Cox, MPR News

item.title: Minnesota ACLU: Local jurisdictions have the right to not cooperate with immigration enforcement
item.description: During President Donald Trump’s first week in office, his new Department of Justice announced plans to potentially criminally prosecute local and state officials who fail to “comply with lawful immigration-related commands and requests.
item.dc:creator: Tom Crann and Ngoc Bui, MPR News

item.title: Medicaid, SNAP, other benefits safe for Nevadans, Lombardo says
item.description: Gov. Joe Lombardo issued a statement Tuesday to clarify what is and is not affected by executive orders issued by President Donald Trump.
item.dc:creator: Greg Haas, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: Trump to sign executive order banning transgender people from military service
item.description: A fact sheet for Monday’s executive order refers to transgender Americans as “trans-identifying,” a technical term used by some to delegitimize transgender identities.
item.dc:creator: Brooke Migdon, Fox 5

item.title: White House says Colombia will take deported migrants after Trump showdown
item.description: The White House said Sunday night that a U.S.-Colombia agreement had come together in the wake of a back-and-forth between the two countries over topics including immigration and tariffs.
item.dc:creator: Tara Suter, Fox 5

item.title: Trump's move to freeze Biden-approved funding draws howls from Democrats
item.description: Democrats are sounding the alarm over President Trump's move to freeze funding approved in two of former President Biden's signature laws. Trump issued an executive order pausing the disbursement of funds appropriated through the Inflation Reduction Act and the Bipartisan Infrastructure Law.
item.dc:creator: Rachel Frazin, Fox 5

item.title: 5 takeaways from Trump's whirlwind week back at the White House
item.description: President Trump stormed into the White House with a whirlwind of a first week, quickly making good on a number of campaign promises while embracing the trappings of the presidency.
item.dc:creator: Alex Gangitano, WFLA, NBC
